question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

shellCommands: "C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"class Route|public function run\\(|public function handle\\(|public function prepare\\(|public function getCallable\\(\" Slim\\Routing\\Route.php"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"class RoutingMiddleware|public function process\\(|public function performRouting\\(|protected function resolveRoutingResultsFromRequest\" Slim\\Middleware\\RoutingMiddleware.php"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"class MiddlewareDispatcher|public function handle\\(|public function add\\(|public function addMiddleware\\(|public function addDeferred\\(|public function addCallable\\(\" Slim\\MiddlewareDispatcher.php"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"class RouteCollectorProxy|public function map\\(|public function get\\(|public function post\\(|public function any\\(|public function group\\(\" Slim\\Routing\\RouteCollectorProxy.php"
graph.answer:
**Central Flow**
1. `AppFactory::create()` / `createFromContainer()` builds the app and wires the core dependencies, including the response factory, container, resolver, collector, router, and middleware dispatcher. Start here if you want the construction path. See [Slim/Factory/AppFactory.php:53](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/Factory/AppFactory.php#L53) and [Slim/Factory/AppFactory.php:77](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/Factory/AppFactory.php#L77).

2. The route-definition API lives on `App` via `RouteCollectorProxy`. Calls like `get()`, `post()`, `any()`, `map()`, and `group()` are just wrappers that forward into the collector. See [Slim/Routing/RouteCollectorProxy.php:109](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/Routing/RouteCollectorProxy.php#L109) and [Slim/Routing/RouteCollectorProxy.php:165](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/Routing/RouteCollectorProxy.php#L165), then the collector creates real route objects in [Slim/Routing/RouteCollector.php:268](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/Routing/RouteCollector.php#L268) and [Slim/Routing/RouteCollector.php:287](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/Routing/RouteCollector.php#L287).

3. `App` is the public runtime entrypoint. `run()` creates a request when none is passed, calls `handle()`, then emits the response. `handle()` is the request-processing API that returns a `ResponseInterface` without sending it. See [Slim/App.php:186](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/App.php#L186) and [Slim/App.php:207](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/App.php#L207).

4. `App::__construct()` seeds the middleware stack with a `RouteRunner` by default. That means explicit `addRoutingMiddleware()` is optional and mainly for ordering control. See [Slim/App.php:55](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/App.php#L55) and [Slim/App.php:125](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/App.php#L125).

5. `App::handle()` delegates to `MiddlewareDispatcher::handle()`, which just calls the current tip of the stack. Middleware is layered last-in-first-out, so the last added middleware runs first. See [Slim/MiddlewareDispatcher.php:71](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/MiddlewareDispatcher.php#L71) and [Slim/MiddlewareDispatcher.php:85](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/MiddlewareDispatcher.php#L85).

6. If routing middleware is present, `RoutingMiddleware::process()` performs routing up front. It stores `RouteContext::ROUTE_PARSER`, `RouteContext::ROUTING_RESULTS`, and on success `RouteContext::ROUTE`. See [Slim/Middleware/RoutingMiddleware.php:42](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/Middleware/RoutingMiddleware.php#L42) and [Slim/Middleware/RoutingMiddleware.php:57](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/Middleware/RoutingMiddleware.php#L57).

7. If routing was not already done, `RouteRunner::handle()` performs it lazily at the end of the stack, then pulls the resolved route from the request and runs it. See [Slim/Routing/RouteRunner.php:57](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/Routing/RouteRunner.php#L57).

8. `Route::run()` appends group middleware once, then hands off to the route’s own dispatcher. `Route::handle()` resolves the callable and executes the invocation strategy, which is where the actual route handler work happens. See [Slim/Routing/Route.php:315](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/Routing/Route.php#L315) and [Slim/Routing/Route.php:342](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/Routing/Route.php#L342).

9. `ResponseEmitter::emit()` is the final I/O step. It writes headers, the status line, and body content to PHP output. See [Slim/ResponseEmitter.php:38](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/Slim/ResponseEmitter.php#L38).

**What To Read Next**
1. Construction and dependency wiring: [tests/Factory/AppFactoryTest.php:153](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/Factory/AppFactoryTest.php#L153), [tests/Factory/AppFactoryTest.php:211](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/Factory/AppFactoryTest.php#L211), [tests/Factory/AppFactoryTest.php:289](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/Factory/AppFactoryTest.php#L289).

2. Main request flow through the app: [tests/AppTest.php:1109](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/AppTest.php#L1109), [tests/AppTest.php:1675](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/AppTest.php#L1675), [tests/AppTest.php:1769](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/AppTest.php#L1769).

3. Middleware ordering and routing middleware insertion: [tests/AppTest.php:594](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/AppTest.php#L594), [tests/AppTest.php:658](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/AppTest.php#L658), [tests/AppTest.php:693](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/AppTest.php#L693).

4. Routing behavior and failure modes: [tests/Middleware/RoutingMiddlewareTest.php:42](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/Middleware/RoutingMiddlewareTest.php#L42), [tests/Middleware/RoutingMiddlewareTest.php:78](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/Middleware/RoutingMiddlewareTest.php#L78), [tests/Middleware/RoutingMiddlewareTest.php:115](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/Middleware/RoutingMiddlewareTest.php#L115), [tests/Middleware/RoutingMiddlewareTest.php:152](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/Middleware/RoutingMiddlewareTest.php#L152).

5. Fallback routing at the kernel tip: [tests/Routing/RouteRunnerTest.php:26](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/Routing/RouteRunnerTest.php#L26).

6. Final output semantics: [tests/ResponseEmitterTest.php:50](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/ResponseEmitterTest.php#L50), [tests/ResponseEmitterTest.php:106](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/ResponseEmitterTest.php#L106), [tests/ResponseEmitterTest.php:124](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/ResponseEmitterTest.php#L124), [tests/ResponseEmitterTest.php:211](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/ResponseEmitterTest.php#L211).

**Two practical gotchas**
1. `run()` emits output; `handle()` does not. If your behavior change needs testable response inspection, prefer `handle()`.
2. `HEAD` requests have their body stripped in `App::handle()`, even if route code wrote content. That behavior is covered in [tests/AppTest.php:1769](/C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/slim/tests/AppTest.php#L1769).

If you want, I can turn this into a “read in this order” checklist for a first patch, or trace one narrower path next, like `GET /route` resolution, middleware ordering, or response emission.
